name: Four Seasons Resort Maui at Wailea

content.verdict: The Four Seasons Maui at Wailea is the gold standard for luxury honeymoons in Hawaii, consistently ranking among the best hotels in the world. Set on the soft golden sands of Wailea Beach, it delivers impeccable service, three stunning pools, and a level of culinary excellence rarely matched on the islands. It's ideal for couples who want to be thoroughly pampered and have easy access to one of Maui's most beautiful stretches of coastline — the only caveat being that the price tag is among the highest in Hawaii.
content.best_room: Book the Ocean View Suite or, if budget allows, the Oceanfront Suite on a high floor. These rooms offer unobstructed views of the Pacific, a deep soaking tub perfect for two, and a furnished lanai where you can watch the sunset with a glass of champagne. The suite-level amenities — including preferred pool cabana access and express check-in — elevate the experience significantly.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Sunset Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive to a lei greeting and be escorted to your ocean-view room already stocked with fresh tropical flowers. Spend the afternoon unwinding on Wailea Beach just steps from the hotel, then head to Spago for a sunset dinner on the lanai — the macadamia-crusted fresh catch is a must-order on your first night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Pool Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Start with a couples' massage at Spa at Four Seasons, which uses locally sourced ingredients like Maui lavender and kukui nut oil. Spend the afternoon at the adults-preferred Serenity Pool, a quieter retreat from the main pools, then end the evening with cocktails at Ferraro's Bar e Ristorante as the sun dips below the horizon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Road to Hana Adventure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rent a car and tackle the iconic Road to Hana, stopping at Twin Falls, the black sand beach at Waianapanapa State Park, and the lush Bamboo Forest. Pack a picnic from the hotel's culinary team and find a quiet waterfall pool to yourselves — the hotel concierge can mark the lesser-known gems on a custom map.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkel & Sailing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Book the hotel's curated snorkeling excursion to Molokini Crater, a partially submerged volcanic caldera teeming with tropical fish and sea turtles. Return by mid-afternoon and revive with a post-snorkel couple's treatment at the spa, focusing on deep hydration after a day in the salt water.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Upcountry Maui & Haleakala
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rise before dawn for the sunrise at Haleakala summit — the Four Seasons concierge can arrange a private sunrise tour with blankets and hot drinks. Descend through Upcountry Maui, stopping at organic farms and the charming town of Makawao for lunch before a leisurely afternoon back at the resort.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Culinary Indulgence & Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Request an in-room breakfast on your lanai, watching early morning surfers below. Spend a slow morning on the beach, then book the Chef's Table experience at Ferraro's Bar e Ristorante for a private, multi-course Italian dinner crafted just for the two of you as torches light the oceanfront terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Savor a final breakfast at the hotel's Duo restaurant, which serves an outstanding Hawaiian buffet with fresh-squeezed juices and made-to-order eggs. Take one last walk along Wailea Beach Path before checking out, and let the concierge arrange a seamless lei farewell for the drive to the airport.

caveats: At $1,200–$3,500 per night, this is one of the most expensive hotels in Hawaii — budget accordingly for a minimum 5-night stay to truly absorb the experience.
The resort is not adults-only, and during school holidays the pool areas can be lively with families — request the Serenity Pool for a quieter escape.
Dining here is exceptional but expensive; factor in $200–$400 per couple per meal at Spago or Ferraro's to avoid sticker shock.

faqs.question: Is the Four Seasons Maui good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — it is consistently ranked among the top honeymoon hotels in the United States. The combination of Wailea Beach frontage, a world-class spa, multiple restaurants by Wolfgang Puck, and the Four Seasons' legendary personalized service creates an atmosphere purpose-built for romance. The hotel also offers dedicated honeymoon packages with champagne, flower petals, and private dining arrangements.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: April through June and September through November offer the ideal balance of warm weather, manageable crowds, and lower rates relative to peak season. July and August are peak family season and the most expensive months. Maui's south shore is generally drier and sunnier than the north, making Wailea an excellent year-round destination.

faqs.question: Is the beach at the Four Seasons Maui good for swimming?
faqs.answer: Wailea Beach is one of the best swimming beaches on Maui — calm, protected waters with excellent visibility and consistent conditions. The hotel's beach attendants set up complimentary loungers and umbrellas, and snorkel gear is available for rent. Sea turtles are regularly spotted just offshore, making it magical even without a boat trip.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Reserve your room as early as possible, ideally 6–12 months out for peak dates. Book the Spa at Four Seasons couples treatments at the time of reservation — peak slots fill up fast. Spago and Ferraro's dinner reservations should also be made before arrival. The concierge can arrange sunset sails, helicopter tours, and Road to Hana private guides.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, the Four Seasons Maui is a full-service family resort, but it does have a dedicated Serenity Pool reserved for guests 18 and over. Honeymooners should request rooms on upper floors and away from family villas for the most peaceful experience.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Kahului Airport (OGG) on Maui, which has direct flights from major US hubs including Los Angeles, San Francisco, Seattle, and Dallas. The drive to the hotel in Wailea is approximately 30 minutes. The hotel can arrange private car transfers, or you can rent a car — recommended for excursions to Hana and Haleakala.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Four Seasons Resort Maui at Wailea

Dear Four Seasons Maui team,

My partner and I are planning our honeymoon and are very interested in staying with you from [DATE] to [DATE] ([X] nights).

We are particularly interested in: an Oceanfront Suite or Ocean View Suite, ideally on an upper floor.

Could you please let us know:
- Current availability for our dates
- Any honeymoon packages or complimentary upgrades available
- Whether the Serenity Pool can be reserved for a private morning session

Thank you for your time.

[Your names]
